note: Authoritative local data: GemRadar gems.db (CoinGecko fundamentals + DeFiLlama protocol fees/TVL/revenue) over the longshot.db price/supply/coil backbone. FUNDAMENTALS-FIRST, low-cap gems ($5M-$250M). Raw signals, NO composite score. null = NO DATA (do not guess). Layers: GATES qualify (G1 real-yield, G2 clean-float, G3 liquidity, G4 not-wash), DIVERGENCES are early-tells (trend halves light up after >=2 daily collects), TRAPS flag distribution. Percentiles are sector-relative. coil_lookalikes (longshot winner-DNA) = the TIMING overlay, not the thesis.

events.subject: Sahara AI 1.03B token unlock
events.note: ~30% of released supply; direct supply shock into a 30d capitulation low

unlock_calendar.note: Curated dated catalysts (edit catalysts.json). DeFiLlama unlocks API is Pro-gated; the routine news-scan supplements. Unlock cliffs feed the G2/D4/T2 logic.

narrative.method: CoinGecko category momentum (24h mcap change + constituent 7d breadth/median); thematic narratives only (chain-ecosystem/stablecoin categories excluded)

leaders.name: Portfolio of Risk-adjusted Investment Strategy Mix

coil_lookalikes.method: validated winner-DNA coil screen (deep-below-high + oversold + at-30d-low [all validated, hold OOS] + below-ATH/high-vol/mid-float/small-cap); primed-but-not-yet-moved. NECESSARY-not-sufficient setup — needs an event trigger.
coil_lookalikes.how_to_use: Beaten-down coils matching the data-validated winner DNA. SETUPS, not buys: most never move. Edge comes ONLY from confluence — a lookalike that is also trending / in a heating narrative / has a near catalyst / has fresh dev-gov.

signal_glossary.deep_below_60d_high: Trading <50% of its 60-day high — deeply beaten down. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.oversold_30d: Down >25% over 30 days — oversold. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample).
signal_glossary.at_30d_low: Trading within ~3% of its 30-day low RIGHT NOW — the capitulation launch zone. VALIDATED (held out-of-sample); a bounce off the low actually LOWERS winner odds, so this is a timing edge.
signal_glossary.oversold_90d: Down >40% over 90 days — sustained downtrend (supporting context).
signal_glossary.deep_below_ath: Trading <15% of all-time high — far from prior peak, room to recover.
signal_glossary.high_vol: Elevated 30d volatility — capable of large moves.
signal_glossary.mid_float: Meaningful but not-fully-diluted float — the winner sweet spot.
signal_glossary.small_cap: Sub-$100M market cap — small enough to multiply.

dev_governance.note: Pre-announcement layer: GitHub release freshness (candidate set, capped) + Snapshot governance (gov_sources.json). Commit velocity for the full universe is in tokens[].gh_commits_4w (CoinGecko). A fresh_release or active proposal precedes the marketing post — read release_notes/body and judge what it does. No score.

dev.release_notes:
The v1.2.23 release of the Nym Wallet

The full list of changes is in the [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/nymtech/nym/blob/develop/nym-wallet/CHANGELOG.md#v1223-2026-06-24)

# Changelog
### General
- [03ffa7b](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/03ffa7b)  shared/dbutil: make database connection parameters configurable per database
- [7781bdb](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/7781bdb)  build: add storagenode-modular and satellite-modular to the release binaries
- [5733dc6](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/5733dc6)  scripts/release: fix glob matching storagenode-modular in storagenode upload
- [6ac3434](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/6ac3434)  scripts/release: add storagenode-updater to release binaries
- [e6fe43a](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/e6fe43a)  shared/modular/cli: fix []string config values from YAML lists
### Satellite
- [03f43d7](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/03f43d7)  satellite: trigger large inv event for open invoices
- [a93ab0f](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/a93ab0f)  satellite/metabase: implement TiDBAdapter IterateExpiredObjects and IterateZombieObjects
- [e0a878b](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/e0a878b)  satellite/metabase: implement (*TiDBAdapter).BucketEmpty
- [939d74d](https://github.com/storj/storj/commit/939d74d)  satellite/metabase: implement (*TiDBAdapter).DeleteObjectExact*
- …[truncated]

## What's Changed
* fix(mobile-iap): checkout below total + 225-credit price cap by @leanmendoza in https://github.com/decentraland/marketplace/pull/2654


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/decentraland/marketplace/compare/8.26.0...8.26.1

Verified and tested stable version of the TAP Protocol implementation.

This version fixes a security issue with "token-trade" that previously allowed to overprint tokens. From block 954543 onward, token-trade is discontinued. 

Use this pre-indexed redb file to save indexing time: https://legacy.trac.network/ord-tap.redb

## Key changes
**Sui gRPC support**
This release updates the Sui watcher to use Sui's new gRPC service, as json-rpc is being deprecated at the end of June 2026. For a migration guide, refer to https://docs.sui.io/develop/accessing-data/json-rpc-migration.

Guardians must update their `suiRPC` config option to point to the new gRPC service on their Sui nodes.

**Arc support**
This release adds support for Arc testnet and mainnet. To enable mainnet, use the following config options:

```
--arcRPC YOUR_RPC
--arcContract 0xC8aD24fC6063c41cB5C12a8e3851AafC3b3CF027 //this is MAINNET
```

**Tempo mainnet support**
This release adds support for Tempo mainnet. To enable it, use the following config options:

```
--tempoRPC YOUR_RPC
--tempoContract 0xbebdb6C8ddC678FfA9f8748f85C815C556Dd8ac6 //this is MAINNET
```

**Tron testnet support**
This release adds support for Tron testnet and mainnet. These release notes will be updated once the mainnet contract is deployed.

```
--tronRPC YOUR_RPC
--tronContract 0x294b5510a771111df96acbc08515678edf0f83e0 //this is TESTNET
```

## What's Changed
* XRPL Watcher and Manager Service support by @evan-gray in …[truncated]

## Description

This is a runtime-only release. It reduces the collator-selection pallet's `MinCandidates` configuration value from 5 to 1 for the Astar and Shiden runtimes. Node clients are not impacted.

This is the release for version v2.6.0.

It includes:
- citrea-v2.6.0-linux-amd64
- citrea-v2.6.0-linux-arm64
- citrea-v2.6.0-osx-arm64
- citrea-cli-v2.6.0-linux-amd64
- citrea-cli-v2.6.0-linux-arm64
- citrea-cli-v2.6.0-osx-arm64
- SHA256SUMS.txt
- SHA256SUMS.txt.sigstore.json

### Highlights

This is a regular minor release.

### Compatibility Table

| Network | Consensus Version        | Minimal CKB Version |
|---------|------------------------- | ------------------- |
| dev     | ckb2023 since epoch 0    |                     |
| preview | ckb2023 since epoch 0    | v0.119.0            |
| testnet | ckb2023 since epoch 9,690 | v0.119.0           |
| mainnet | ckb2023 since epoch 12,293 | v0.200.0          |

See [how to connect to various chains](https://github.com/nervosnetwork/ckb/wiki/Chains) in Wiki.

### Default assume valid target

- Mainnet: [0x506cc9ae64f577dd41b57d5860600334132cf502cabdff5a9cc2285c81fb8520](https://explorer.nervos.org/block/0x506cc9ae64f577dd41b57d5860600334132cf502cabdff5a9cc2285c81fb8520)
- Testnet: [0xb5b26b01669c4288938759c97738e07a5a7a03a07c77a518db6d3913b034c0a5](https://pudge.explorer.nervos.org/block/0xb5b26b01669c4288938759c97738e07a5a7a03a07c77a518db6d3913b034c0a5)

### Downloads

| OS                | Arch  | Runtime Dependencies | Package                 | Sign                |
| ----------------- | ----- | -------------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------- |
| macOS       …[truncated]

```
$ docker pull graphprotocol/graph-node:v0.44.0
```

### Critical Fix

- **`EntityCache::load_related` returned wrong derived-collection membership on same-block parent reassignment.** Membership was decided against intermediate cache layers rather than the entity's final state, so entities reassigned to a new parent within the same block could be left in the old collection, omitted from the new one, or wrongly retained after a cross-handler revert. See Upgrade Notes. ([#6548](https://github.com/graphprotocol/graph-node/pull/6548))

### Breaking Changes

- **Elasticsearch CLI flags removed.** `--elasticsearch-url`, `--elasticsearch-user`, `--elasticsearch-password` (and the matching `ELASTICSEARCH_*` env vars) are gone. Configure Elasticsearch under the new `[log_store]` section in `graph-node.toml`. See Upgrade Notes. ([#6278](https://github.com/graphprotocol/graph-node/pull/6278))

### What's New

- **Query subgraph logs over GraphQL.** A new `_logs` field exposes subgraph-emitted logs (mapping `log.*` calls, runtime, system) with filters for level, timestamp range, and text search, plus pagination and `orderDirection`. The old Elasticsearch sink for subgraph …[truncated]

🚨 **Note - some users have experienced incompatibility issues with older CPUs that do not support newer instruction sets; we are currently investigating** 🚨 

The o1Labs team is proud to announce that `3.4.0` is ready for use on Mainnet! `3.4.0` is planned to be the last standard Berkeley release before we approach the Mesa era! This release includes a large roll up of fixes and improvements to the Mina Protocol as well as our CI and infrastructure that will be more efficiently utilised as we subsequently run through the Mesa Upgrade process in the near future.

If you run into any issues, please include `Release: 3.4.0 Stable` in the name of any issues you create. Please report any issues you encounter [here](https://github.com/MinaProtocol/mina/issues/new/choose) or join [the discussion](https://github.com/MinaProtocol/mina/discussions/18877)!

**Some highlights of what has changed**

**New Features**
- #17954: Bump RocksDB to 10.5.1
- #18230: Enhance multi-key-file-storage library with new features
- #18527: Support mixed hf mode

**Improvements**
- #18275: Update Rust version to 1.92.0
- #18170: Bump up proof-systems to release 0.2.0
- #18270: Fix empty Mesa …[truncated]

This is a patch release of dcrd which includes the following changes:

- Correct a potential node crash that only affects the v2.1.4 release
- Coins excluded from a mixing session that exceeds new limits are no longer greylisted

## Changelog

This patch release consists of 4 commits from 2 contributors which total to 5 files changed, 36 additional lines of code, and 35 deleted lines of code.

All commits since the last release may be viewed on GitHub [here](https://github.com/decred/dcrd/compare/release-v2.1.4...release-v2.1.5).

### Protocol and network:

- [release-v2.1] server: Revert Don't consider services before handshake ([decred/dcrd#3670](https://github.com/decred/dcrd/pull/3670))

### Mixing message relay (mix pool):

- [release-v2.1] mixpool: Avoid greylist strikes on size excluded peers ([decred/dcrd#3675](https://github.com/decred/dcrd/pull/3675))

### Developer-related module management:

- [release-v2.1] main: Use backported mixing updates ([decred/dcrd#3675](https://github.com/decred/dcrd/pull/3675))

### Misc:

- [release-v2.1] release: Bump for 2.1.5 ([decred/dcrd#3671](https://github.com/decred/dcrd/pull/3671))

### Code Contributors …[truncated]

✨ UI for Rate Card Change
✨ Basic Uphold Connection KPIs (Daily Metrics)
🐛 Enable a 5 BAT threshold for nagging users about going through KYC process. (Previously we emailed anyone with a positive balance)
🐛 Fix logic error with Statements
🐛   Rolling development logs. Locally no more GBs of logs
